methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Late Stage → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.2x) to $2.0B last post money.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 20 same-sector + similar-valuation-tier peers in Space & Aerospace. Range: $400M - $8.0B.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Space & Aerospace P/S median is 36.9x across 6 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

data.narrative:
Vast is a late-stage Space & Aerospace company currently valued at $2.0B post-money, having raised $300M+. The valuation assessment draws from a peer universe of 20 same-sector comparable companies across similar valuation tiers, supplemented by 5 public Space & Aerospace comps (RKLB, PL, LUNR, ASTS, SATL) and 8 private peers including Sierra Space ($8.0B), Relativity Space ($4.2B), and Astranis ($3.5B).

Two valuation methods are applicable. Stage-progression analysis applies a 1.2x step-up multiplier typical for Late Stage advancement, yielding $2.4B. Peer-median triangulation across the 20-company cohort returns $1.4B, with the comparable set ranging from $400M to $8.0B. Public-comp anchoring via P/S multiples cannot be applied due to absence of target revenue disclosure. The median of these two methods produces $1.9B; the low-to-high range spans $1.2B to $2.8B.

The $2.0B current valuation sits slightly above the $1.9B median implied value and within the derived range, supported by the company's late-stage standing and $300M+ capital raised. Key uncertainties include revenue trajectory and capital efficiency relative to the peer set, which spans an eight-fold valuation spread ($400M–$8.0B). The analysis supports valuations between $1.2B and $2.8B, with $1.9B as the central estimate.
